decontamination.skin_decon: Remove clothing, wash patient with soap and water. Universal precautions.

management_steps.details: Remove clothing, wash patient with soap and water. Universal precautions.
General management principles of a poisoned patient
0.04-0.05 mg/kg IV, double every 5 minutes until atropinisation (clearing of respiratory secretions)
Once atropinised, start infusion at 10-20% of total atropinisation dose per hour
Check blood glucose. Use benzodiazepine only (Clonazepam, Diazepam, Lorazepam). NO anticonvulsant agents.
If significant weakness present, consider pralidoxime (PAM)

management_steps.caution: Must be given within 24-48h of exposure for OP. Not for carbamates.

management_steps.details: Continuous cardiac monitoring, serial cholinesterase levels
management_steps.caution: Re-intoxication can occur with fat-soluble OPs due to redistribution

drugs.notes: Until atropinised (clear secretions). May need massive doses
For nicotinic effects. Within 24-48h of OP exposure. NOT for carbamates

protocols.notes: DUMBELS mnemonic for muscarinic effects | SLUDGE mnemonic also used | Red cell cholinesterase more accurate than plasma | Carbamates typically self-limiting (reversible inhibition) | Lipid-soluble OPs (fenthion, chlorfenthion) can cause delayed toxicity due to redistribution from adipose tissue
